result.stdout:
Description:
  Telerik CLI - Unified command-line interface for Telerik products and services

Usage:
  Telerik.CLI [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  license          License-related commands.
  mcp              MCP configuration commands.
  nuget            NuGet configuration commands.
  login            Login via browser and persist login session.
  logout           Clear the persisted login session.
  whoami           Print basic info about the current login session.
  setup <product>  Run the full Telerik developer-machine setup: login -> NuGet config -> license key -> MCP config.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Fetch and save Telerik license key.

Usage:
  Telerik.CLI license get-key [options]

Options:
  --json          Output result as JSON.
  --no-browser    Skip the browser-based login and use manual redirect URL entry instead. Use this in environments where the loopback redirect is blocked (e.g. firewall, antivirus, WSL, corporate proxy).
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  MCP configuration commands.

Usage:
  Telerik.CLI mcp [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  config <product>  Register MCP servers in IDE configuration files. Omit --ide and product to configure all combinations.

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  Telerik.CLI mcp [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  config <product>  Register MCP servers in IDE configuration files. Omit --ide and product to configure all combinations.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Register MCP servers in IDE configuration files. Omit --ide and product to configure all combinations.

Usage:
  Telerik.CLI mcp config [<product>] [options]

Arguments:
  <product>  Product to register. Known values: blazor. Omit to register all products.

Options:
  --json          Output result as JSON.
  --ide <ide>     Target IDE. Known values: visualstudio, vscode, cursor. Omit to target all IDEs.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

commands.payload:

  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:

commands.payload:
Description:
  NuGet configuration commands.

Usage:
  Telerik.CLI nuget [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  config  Configure Telerik NuGet package source.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Configure Telerik NuGet package source.

Usage:
  Telerik.CLI nuget config [options]

Options:
  --json               Output result as JSON.
  --scope <scope>      Scope: 'user' or 'project'. [default: user]
  --path <path>        Path to NuGet.config or project directory (required for project scope).
  --api-key <api-key>  Telerik NuGet API key (auto-generated if not provided).
  --force              Override existing credentials even if already configured.
  --no-browser         Skip the browser-based login and use manual redirect URL entry instead. Use this in environments where the loopback redirect is blocked (e.g. firewall, antivirus, WSL, corporate proxy).
  -?, -h, --help       Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Configure Telerik NuGet package source.

Usage:
  Telerik.CLI nuget config [options]

Options:
  --json               Output result as JSON.
  --scope <scope>      Scope: 'user' or 'project'. [default: user]
  --path <path>        Path to NuGet.config or project directory (required for project scope).
  --api-key <api-key>  Telerik NuGet API key (auto-generated if not provided).
  --force              Override existing credentials even if already configured.
  --no-browser         Skip the browser-based login and use manual redirect URL entry instead. Use this in environments where the loopback redirect is blocked (e.g. firewall, antivirus, WSL, corporate proxy).
  -?, -h, --help       Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Run the full Telerik developer-machine setup: login -> NuGet config -> license key -> MCP config.

Usage:
  Telerik.CLI setup [<product>] [options]

Arguments:
  <product>  Product to register for MCP. Known values: blazor. Omit to register all products.

Options:
  --json                     Output result as JSON.
  --scope <scope>            NuGet config scope: 'user' or 'project'. [default: user]
  --nuget-path <nuget-path>  Path to NuGet.config or project directory (required when --scope project).
  --force                    Override existing NuGet credentials even if already configured.
  --no-browser               Skip the browser-based login and use manual redirect URL entry instead. Use this in environments where the loopback redirect is blocked (e.g. firewall, antivirus, WSL, corporate proxy).
  --interactive              Run the setup flow interactively, prompting for each step. Each prompt shows its default value; pressing Enter accepts it. This is the default when stdin is a terminal.
  --no-interactive           Run the setup flow non-interactively, relying on explicit options or their built-in defaults. This is the default when stdin is redirected (e.g. in CI).
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Run the full Telerik developer-machine setup: login -> NuGet config -> license key -> MCP config.

Usage:
  Telerik.CLI setup [<product>] [options]

Arguments:
  <product>  Product to register for MCP. Known values: blazor. Omit to register all products.

Options:
  --json                     Output result as JSON.
  --scope <scope>            NuGet config scope: 'user' or 'project'. [default: user]
  --nuget-path <nuget-path>  Path to NuGet.config or project directory (required when --scope project).
  --force                    Override existing NuGet credentials even if already configured.
  --no-browser               Skip the browser-based login and use manual redirect URL entry instead. Use this in environments where the loopback redirect is blocked (e.g. firewall, antivirus, WSL, corporate proxy).
  --interactive              Run the setup flow interactively, prompting for each step. Each prompt shows its default value; pressing Enter accepts it. This is the default when stdin is a terminal.
  --no-interactive           Run the setup flow non-interactively, relying on explicit options or their built-in defaults. This is the default when stdin is redirected (e.g. in CI).
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information
